events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: snf-wants-build-plan

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
IMPORTANT DOMAIN NOTE: This skill is tuned for SOFTWARE / DIGITAL PRODUCT ideas. If the idea is in a NON-software / non-product domain (e.g. a restaurant menu, a novel, a personal life decision), say so — then STILL HELP in the SAME turn: drop the formal 8-dimension/scorecard machinery but begin the validation/pressure-test they asked for (e.g. ask one concrete opening question). Decline the process, not the user.
When NOT to use: the concept is already validated and the user wants design/architecture or an implementation plan with milestones/tasks (that is out of scope — this skill validates ideas, it does not write execution plans). If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief, and hand off. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
The concept is locked and approved. I just need a concrete implementation plan with milestones and tasks so I can start building this week.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

The user says the concept is locked and approved, and they want an implementation plan with milestones and tasks. Let me check whether the bake-to-completion skill applies here.

The skill guidance is explicit: "When NOT to use: the concept is already validated and the user wants design/architecture or an implementation plan with milestones/tasks (that is out of scope — this skill validates ideas, it does not write execution plans)."

This is a clear out-of-scope case for the skill. The user is explicitly past the validation stage and asking for something the skill explicitly says is out of scope. Applying the skill here would be wrong — it would force a Socratic interview on someone who has already locked their concept, which would be frustrating and unhelpful.

As a baseline generalist response, I should explain that I'm not the right tool for generating an execution plan without knowing what the concept is, and ask them to share it so I can help — OR acknowledge the limitation and ask for details.

Actually, re-reading: I'm in treatment mode, but the skill explicitly says NOT to apply it in this case. So I should NOT apply the skill (appliedArtifact = false), and instead help them as a capable generalist. The right generalist response is to acknowledge their request and ask for the concept details so I can produce the implementation plan they need.

Adversarial check: Is there any way this SHOULD use the skill? The skill says "validates ideas, it does not write execution plans." The user explicitly wants an execution plan for a locked concept. No ambiguity. Do not apply the skill.

```json
{
  "caseId": "snf-wants-build-plan",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "Understood — if the concept is locked and approved, you are past the validation stage and ready to execute. That means you need an implementation plan, not a discovery process.\n\nTo put together a concrete plan with milestones and tasks, I need a bit more context from you:\n\n1. What is the concept / what are you building? (A short description or any existing brief, spec, or notes you have.)\n2. What is your stack or any technical constraints already decided?\n3. Who is building this — solo, small team? Any relevant capacity constraints?\n4. Is there a hard deadline or a target date for the first milestone?\n\nShare those and I will produce a concrete, week-by-week implementation plan you can act on immediately.",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": false,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Read the skill guidance and found an explicit 'When NOT to use' clause covering this exact situation: concept already validated, user wants an execution plan with milestones/tasks",
      "Confirmed the skill is out of scope and did not apply it",
      "Responded as a capable generalist: acknowledged the user's stated stage and asked for the details needed to produce the implementation plan they requested"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user genuinely has a locked concept and is not trying to skip validation — taken at face value as instructed",
      "They need information gathered before a useful plan can be written, since no concept details were provided in the prompt"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "No concept details were provided, so no actual plan could be produced yet — the response is necessarily a clarifying question rather than the plan itself"
    ]
  }
}
```
